config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent Indonesia vs China native-or-English block. Each persona speaks only English or its own native language.
config.cell_description: One-side-native cross cell: Indonesia persona speaks Indonesian; China persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: One-side-native cross cell: Indonesia persona speaks Indonesian; China persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu harus menjadi dasar yang dilindungi dulu, karena kalau kepentingan masyarakat selalu diutamakan tanpa batas, hak minoritas dan orang yang lemah bisa mudah dikorbankan. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi seharusnya dicapai lewat pembatasan yang sangat jelas dan adil, bukan dengan menekan kebebasan pribadi secara berlebihan. Dalam pandangan saya, masyarakat yang sehat justru dibangun ketika hak individu dihormati dan kepentingan umum dijaga secara seimbang.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Society should matter more in some cases, but individual rights must not be treated as disposable. From my perspective in China, people are often taught to value harmony, stability, and collective responsibility, and I understand why that feels important, especially in a large society where disorder can hurt everyone. But I do not think that gives the state or the majority a blank check to override personal freedom, because once “society” becomes the excuse for everything, minorities and ordinary people can be silenced too easily. The right balance is not simple individualism, but strong limits on collective power so that public interests are pursued without crushing personal dignity.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan atas hak individu secara umum. Dari pengalaman Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan bersama” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membenarkan pembatasan yang malah merugikan kelompok kecil, warga biasa, atau orang yang tidak punya suara. Kepentingan publik memang penting, tetapi harus dibatasi oleh hak dasar yang jelas; kalau tidak, atas nama stabilitas atau harmoni, kebebasan pribadi bisa dikorbankan terlalu mudah. Menurut saya, masyarakat yang sehat justru yang berani melindungi individu, karena dari situlah keadilan sosial bisa tumbuh.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should automatically take priority over the individual, because in practice that idea can become a very convenient excuse for abuse. From my perspective in China, I understand why people emphasize harmony, stability, and the common good, but I have also seen how easily “for the public interest” can be used to silence ordinary people or ignore weak groups. Public interests matter, especially in crises, but they should have clear limits and real accountability, otherwise the collective becomes a tool of power rather than a protection for society. A healthy society is not one that sacrifices individuals first; it is one that protects individual dignity while still asking people to contribute to the common good.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak pernyataan itu karena di Indonesia alasan “demi masyarakat” terlalu mudah dipakai untuk menekan orang kecil dan minoritas. Kepentingan umum memang penting, tetapi tidak boleh ditempatkan di atas hak individu secara otomatis, karena tanpa batas yang jelas itu bisa berubah jadi pembenaran untuk penyalahgunaan kuasa. Dalam pengalaman saya, justru ketika hak dasar dilindungi, orang lebih berani berpartisipasi dan masyarakat jadi lebih sehat. Jadi, bagi saya, yang harus diprioritaskan adalah keseimbangan yang tegas: hak individu sebagai pagar utama, lalu kepentingan sosial dijalankan di dalam pagar itu.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not accept the statement as written, because putting society above the individual too easily turns into a license for abuse. From my perspective in China, collective goals like stability and harmony do matter, and I understand why many people value them in a large and densely populated society, but I have also seen how “for the common good” can be used to silence ordinary people and weaker groups. The best approach is not to rank society above rights by default, but to protect individual dignity as the baseline and only limit it with clear, accountable, and narrow reasons. If society is healthy, it should be strong enough to serve people, not strong enough to crush them.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
